\noindent We start with setting up a cvs\_obj object, and fill it with the parameters. We use the prefix \ttblue{ar} for accounts receivable.

\par\addvspace{\topsep}
\begingroup
\fvset{listparameters={%
  \setlength{\topsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\partopsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\parsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\itemsep}{0pt}%
}}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_blue}]
arSample <- cvs_obj(bv = accounts_receivable$amount,
                    id = accounts_receivable$invoice,
                    n = 100)
\end{Verbatim}
\endgroup
\par\addvspace{\topsep}
\noindent We select the sample, using a seed equal to 1.
